mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate over recent months signals easier financial conditions and a lower equity discount rate, which should lift S&P 500 prices over the next 20 trading days. The signal is falsified if rate cuts do not coincide with positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered easing episodes do not produce positive 20d returns more often than not, or if the average 20d forward return after FEDFUNDS declines is negative.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE above its recent baseline signals labor-market deterioration and weaker household income growth, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect is strongest when unemployment is still rising rather than merely elevated.
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE rise triggers are followed by positive 20d S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the mean 20d return after the trigger is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and PAYEMS both improve together, the market should read that as broadening final demand and stabilizing labor income, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The joint condition is necessary because sales growth without payroll gains is not enough to confirm demand strength.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous upward moves in RSAFS and PAYEMS fail to deliver positive 20d returns on average, or if either series alone explains the signal as well as the pair.

mechanism_narrative: A renewed decline in INDPRO indicates ongoing industrial contraction and weak current output, which should keep S&P 500 forward returns negative over a 20-day horizon. This mechanism is falsified if production weakness is not associated with market declines.
failure_condition: Reject if two-month INDPRO declines are followed by positive 20d S&P 500 returns in a majority of cases, or if average returns after the trigger are not negative.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in HOUST reflects collapsing housing demand and a negative wealth and credit signal, which should pressure S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal is most informative when housing activity is falling from an already weak level.
failure_condition: Reject if large HOUST declines are not followed by negative 60d S&P 500 returns on average, or if the hit rate for negative returns is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is still elevated while FEDFUNDS remains high, real policy is restrictive and valuation multiples should compress, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The mechanism fails if high inflation is not paired with policy restraint.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation remains above 3% year over year and FEDFUNDS > 4.5%
failure_condition: Reject if the combined high-inflation/high-rate regime does not precede negative 20d returns, or if the pair adds no explanatory power beyond CPIAUCSL or FEDFUNDS alone.

mechanism_narrative: An outright decline in PAYEMS signals payroll losses and weaker consumer income, which should translate into negative S&P 500 returns over the next week as investors reprice growth expectations quickly. The effect is a fast reaction to labor deterioration.
failure_condition: Reject if monthly PAYEMS declines are not followed by negative 5d S&P 500 returns more often than positive returns, or if average 5d returns are nonnegative.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-crisis rebound, simultaneous gains in RSAFS, HOUST, and INDPRO indicate a broad cyclical demand recovery that should lift S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal requires all three to improve because isolated gains do not confirm a broad expansion.
failure_condition: Reject if the three-series recovery pattern does not produce positive 60d returns on average, or if any single series alone explains the effect as well as the joint pattern.
